codeReview.summary: Commit 7f5bf8a fixes the `getBranch()` masking bug and renames the timeout error to `TimeoutError`, and the added regression tests cover those paths. However, the rerun still does not clear all blocking issues inherited from `git-ops-error-handling`: `AuthError` redaction remains incomplete for credential strings containing `@`, so auth stderr can still leak password fragments.

issues.description: `redactCredentials()` still stops at the first `@` inside the credential segment. With the current regex, `https://admin:p@ssw0rd@git.example.com/repo.git` becomes `https://<redacted>@ssw0rd@git.example.com/repo.git`, so `AuthError.stderr` can still leak part of the password. That means the prior `git-ops-error-handling` blocker about avoiding credential exposure is not fully resolved. The existing regression at `packages/git-ops/test/security.test.ts:393-405` only asserts that the full `p@ssw0rd` token disappears, not that fragments such as `ssw0rd` are removed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's TDD/manual-verification procedure is still not being followed or accurately reported on this fix rerun. The worker edited `packages/git-ops/src/live.ts` before updating/adding tests, and the handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` with no deviations recorded.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-30` requires creating the test file before implementation and `:80-82` requires manual verification for SSH-related features. In the transcript skeleton for worker session `f96755dd-7677-4113-9f86-4bbf9c3967bd`, lines 253-259 show an Edit of `packages/git-ops/src/live.ts` before the later test-update/addition steps at lines 396-400 and 565-567. The handoff file `/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T19-56-21-986Z__fix-git-ops-errors__f96755dd-7677-4113-9f86-4bbf9c3967bd.json:14-27,79-81` records only automated commands and still marks `followedProcedure` true.

summary: Fail. The fix correctly makes `getBranch()` propagate non-detached-HEAD failures and exposes `TimeoutError`, but the rerun still leaves the earlier auth-redaction blocker unresolved: `AuthError` can leak password suffixes when credentials contain `@`, so the prior `git-ops-error-handling` failure is not fully cleared.
